 {"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899416368Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899375451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1365","title":"demo_showcase: Pretty/Inspect scene","description":"Create a scene that demonstrates Pretty + Inspect on real structs used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.747083708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28.157644052Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pretty"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.762578230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-1365","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Use `Pretty` and `Inspect` on *real demo structs* so the output feels practical:
- `Config`
- `DemoState` snapshot
- a fake API request struct

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is readable and styled (e.g., via panels) rather than raw `Debug`.
- Demonstrates both `Pretty::new(&value)` and `inspect(&console, &value)` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1388","title":"demo_showcase: DemoState model","description":"Define DemoState: service statuses, pipeline steps, progress values, log ring buffer, timestamps.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:47.996627782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10.909170452Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:48.012575295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-1388","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Data model

Keep the state small but expressive:
- run id / seed
- start time + elapsed
- services: name -> status (OK/WARN/ERR) + latency + version
- pipeline: ordered stages with progress + status + eta
- logs: ring buffer of recent lines (already formatted strings or structured records)
- current “headline” action text

## Acceptance criteria

- State can be snapshot-read without blocking (Arc<Mutex<…>> or similar).
- State updates drive visible changes in the dashboard each tick.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-13i9","title":"demo_showcase: Scene trait + registry","description":"Define a Scene abstraction (name/summary/run) and a registry for list/select.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.895960587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35.618073665Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scenes"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.908657832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scene API shape

Keep scenes simple and testable. Suggested shape:

- `fn name(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn summary(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn run(&self, console: &Arc<Console>, cfg: &Config) -> Result<(), DemoError>`

A registry returns an ordered list for full runs and a lookup map for `--scene`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--list-scenes` prints name + summary in a nice table (itself a showcase).
- Scene ordering is stable and matches storyboard.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-13i9","title":"demo_showcase: Scene trait + registry","description":"Define a Scene abstraction (name/summary/run) and a registry for list/select.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.895960587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:56.234108601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scenes"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:56.234078766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.908657832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scene API shape

Keep scenes simple and testable. Suggested shape:

- `fn name(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn summary(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn run(&self, console: &Arc<Console>, cfg: &Config) -> Result<(), DemoError>`

A registry returns an ordered list for full runs and a lookup map for `--scene`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--list-scenes` prints name + summary in a nice table (itself a showcase).
- Scene ordering is stable and matches storyboard.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-164e","title":"demo_showcase: export bundle (HTML/SVG)","description":"Add optional HTML/SVG export of the demo output (capture + write files) with clear UX and paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.512659356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156286674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156258251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.540309392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-164e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Support a flag that captures the demo output and writes an export bundle:
- `demo_showcase.html` (inline or external css)
- `demo_showcase.svg`

The export is an important “proof of portability”: output can be embedded in docs, GitHub issues, etc.

## UX expectations

- `--export-dir <path>` writes files there and prints a final summary panel with the paths.
- `--export` writes to a temp dir (like the existing `examples/e2e_export.rs`) and prints the dir.
- Export should be deterministic when `--width/--height` are provided.

## Acceptance criteria

- Export works with `--features full,tracing` (and without).
- The exported HTML/SVG contains representative content (not just a trivial “hello”).
- The demo prints clear instructions for viewing the artifacts.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-17s7","title":"Traceback: add code context rendering (extra_lines)","description":"Improve rich.traceback parity beyond synthetic frame list.

Scope:
- Extend Traceback rendering to optionally include code context lines (extra_lines) for frames when source is available (either from file or provided explicitly).
- Keep deterministic fixtures by allowing frames to carry an optional source snippet.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for rendering with/without code context; width handling.
- Conformance fixture for a controlled traceback that includes code context (plain match; ANSI optional).
- Docs update: FEATURE_PARITY.md moves Traceback closer to Implemented (or notes updated).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:04.702324111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:47.684123808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17s7","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:04.720967934Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-191b","title":"demo_showcase: color downgrade visual check","description":"Verify the palette looks acceptable under forced Standard/EightBit/TrueColor color systems.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:03.637205914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:26.553332176Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["color","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-191b","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:03.652293204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":27,"issue_id":"bd-191b","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What to verify

Run the demo (or `--scene hero`) under forced color systems:
- Standard (16)
- EightBit (256)
- TrueColor

Ensure the palette still communicates hierarchy and status even when downgraded.

## Acceptance criteria

- No “invisible” text (foreground ~= background).
- Status badges remain distinguishable.
- Tables/panels remain readable.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:26Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1by8","title":"demo_showcase: palette + Theme definitions","description":"Define the palette and implement Theme style definitions (named styles used across scenes).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:58.915479693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:13.025051565Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1by8","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:58.927882861Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-1by8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output

- Style names should be descriptive and reusable, e.g.:
  - `brand.title`, `brand.subtitle`
  - `status.ok`, `status.warn`, `status.err`
  - `section.rule`, `panel.title`, `table.header`
  - `log.trace`/`log.debug`/… (even if logger has its own styling)

## Acceptance criteria

- Most scene styling references these names via markup (`[status.ok]OK[/]`) or theme lookup.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1cdx","title":"demo_showcase: timing/performance tuning","description":"Tune timing and refresh rates so the demo feels smooth but not slow; ensure --quick is truly fast.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:25.545745514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:46.774616470Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cdx","depends_on_id":"bd-6ozb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:25.560089945Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-1cdx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Tuning targets

- Default run: “feels animated” but not long (aim ~8–15s interactive).
- `--quick`: near-instant (aim < 3s).
- Refresh rate: cap to something sane (e.g. <= 10 fps).

Ensure sleeps are routed through a single helper so tuning is easy.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gou","title":"demo_showcase: shared Console builder","description":"Build the shared Console from Config (theme, width/height overrides, markup/emoji/links, force_terminal).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.491588228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:53.701008929Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["console","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:51.202039996Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:53.700981077Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gou","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:03.502866179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-1gou","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Console builder responsibilities

- Apply the demo `Theme` (from the design bead).
- Respect width/height overrides for deterministic output.
- Respect `--force-terminal` to enable color in pipes when requested.
- Provide toggles for markup/emoji/links if we expose them.

## Acceptance criteria

- All scenes use the same Console instance (or share an Arc) so output is consistent.
- Console config is derived solely from `Config` (no hidden env-only behavior except documented defaults like `FORCE_COLOR`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:27Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jk8","title":"demo_showcase: scene runner main loop","description":"Implement the scene runner for full sequence + --scene single-run mode; ensure clean termination.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.660057261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:50.455414413Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.672997309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Responsibilities

- Initialize console + theme.
- Decide run mode (full vs single scene).
- Ensure clean teardown for live/screen modes (cursor restored, screen disabled).
- Provide a consistent “section framing” between scenes (rule + title).

## Acceptance criteria

- `--scene hero` runs only that scene and exits 0.
- Full run prints a summary/outro and exits 0.
- Errors are printed via rich_rust (e.g., traceback scene) and still exit non-zero when appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:50Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1jk8","title":"demo_showcase: scene runner main loop","description":"Implement the scene runner for full sequence + --scene single-run mode; ensure clean termination.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.660057261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:08.902529845Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-13i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:06.396819083Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-1gou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:04.087960586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:01.602157779Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:10.672997309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","depends_on_id":"bd-3j1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:08.902502053Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1jk8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Responsibilities

","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o8x","title":"demo_showcase: Config + CLI parser implementation","description":"Implement a minimal CLI parser and Config struct used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.166796577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:48.539112863Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:48.539079260Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:46.096304063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:01.178894306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1o8x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation notes

- Prefer a small `Config` struct with defaults and a `parse_args(Vec<String>) -> Result<Config, String>` style API.
- Keep parsing strict but friendly: unknown flags show error + hint to `--help`.
- Avoid adding heavy dependencies (e.g., clap) for the demo.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--help` prints usage + examples.
- `--list-scenes` and `--scene <name>` parse correctly.
- Boolean flags support both `--foo` and `--no-foo` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ohr","title":"Port rich.pretty + rich.inspect equivalents","description":"Implement Rich-style pretty printing / inspection.

Scope:
- Provide a Pretty renderable for Rust values (Debug) with optional syntax highlighting
- Provide an inspect-like helper that prints type info + fields when possible

Acceptance criteria:
- Pretty output is stable and width-aware
- Unit tests cover nested structs/collections
- Document Rust-idiomatic differences vs Python inspect
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:38:55.795289624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:46:35.895142548Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:46:35.895076846Z","close_reason":"Implemented Debug-based Pretty/Inspect renderables","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ohr","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:39:01.234951263Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pc","title":"Refactor Segment to use Cow<'a, str>","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T01:17:57.773069201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T01:32:28.193304293Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T01:32:28.193244581Z","close_reason":"Completed refactor of Segment to use Cow<'a, str>","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2b0s","title":"demo_showcase: narrative + scene storyboard","description":"Choose the demo storyline, name, copy, and the ordered scene list (full run + single-scene names).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.806761210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217861682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217788947Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.821701513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output deliverable

Produce a concrete storyboard so implementation can follow without ambiguity.

### Recommended scene list (example)

1. `hero` – branding + capabilities + palette preview
2. `dashboard` – live layout with services/pipeline/logs
3. `deep_dive_markdown` – release notes / runbook
4. `deep_dive_syntax` – config/code snippet
5. `deep_dive_json` – API payloads
6. `debug_tools` – pretty/inspect + traceback + logging/tracing
7. `export` – HTML/SVG bundle
8. `outro` – summary table/tree + next steps

### Naming

Pick a fictional product name and keep it consistent in copy, e.g. “Nebula Deploy”.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each scene has a 1–2 sentence purpose description.
- Each scene lists which rich_rust features it must exercise.
- Single-scene mode (`--scene <name>`) uses these names verbatim.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cjt","title":"demo_showcase: interactive start wizard","description":"Add an optional interactive start wizard using Prompt (scenario/speed/export choices).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.004600090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:31.580966765Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","prompt"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.017001830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Wizard questions (suggested)

- Export: yes/no

Keep it short and skippable (defaults).

 {"id":"bd-2fl5","title":"demo_showcase: visual design system (theme/palette)","description":"Define a cohesive theme/palette and named styles that make the demo look like a real product.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:17.976813041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788393345Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788358650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:18.006687094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the demo visually consistent and “brandable” using the Theme system and rich_rust style primitives. This is where we turn the output from “works” into “wow”.

## Design principles

- Use a small, consistent palette: primary/accent/success/warn/error + neutrals.
- Prefer named styles via `Theme` so users can copy patterns (`status.ok`, `status.warn`, `panel.title`, etc.).
- Keep readability on low-color terminals: ensure acceptable downgrades to 16/256 colors.
- Use whitespace, rules, and alignment to create hierarchy (like a UI, not a log dump).

## Deliverables

- A `Theme::from_style_definitions(...)` set used by the demo’s Console.
- Named styles for:
  - headers / hero
  - section rules
  - table header + key columns
  - status badges (OK/WARN/ERR)
  - log levels
  - “call to action” links
- Optional: a small helper to render “badges” like `[bold white on green] OK [/ ]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- The demo uses named styles in *most* scenes (not just ad-hoc `Style::parse`).
- The demo still looks coherent when forced to `ColorSystem::Standard`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2hba","title":"demo_showcase: UI helper primitives","description":"Implement small UI helpers (badges, section headers, key/value rows) using Text/Style.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.342641761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:19.519656844Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.357952838Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2hba","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Helpers to build

- `badge(text, style_name)` -> `Text` (or markup snippet)
- `kv_row(key, value)` -> renderable suitable for panels
- `section_title("...")` -> rule + aligned title

These keep the demo consistent and prevent copy/paste styling drift.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2wxz","title":"demo_showcase: controlled failure narrative","description":"Define the ‘failure’ moment in the narrative (what fails, why) and ensure it drives useful output (logs + traceback + inspect).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.641139331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:44.867480530Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.656509441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Purpose

The “failure moment” is what makes the demo feel real: it shows how rich_rust helps when things go wrong.

## Requirements

- The failure must be believable and related to earlier output (services/pipeline/config).
- It must justify showing: logs, inspect/pretty, and traceback.
- After demonstrating the failure, the demo should recover and show a final summary (unless `--fail-fast`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2xsi","title":"demo_showcase: observability + debugging scenes","description":"Exercise RichLogger (+ RichTracingLayer), Pretty/Inspect, and Traceback in a believable error/debug workflow.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:27.657814798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:44.770843850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:44.770805709Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:42.336767127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:27.669647259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Story beat

After the dashboard “run” starts, introduce a controlled failure (e.g. service returns 500 / config invalid) and show how rich_rust helps debug:

- **Logging**: RichLogger formatted lines, keyword highlighting, hyperlinks to file paths
- **Tracing** (feature `tracing`): span/event demo integrated into the same output stream
- **Pretty/Inspect**: show the request/config struct being inspected
- **Traceback**: print a Rich-style exception traceback with synthetic frames

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is clearly more readable than raw logs (level colors, timestamps, structured hints).
- If `tracing` feature is not enabled, the tracing scene prints a small “disabled” message and continues.
- Traceback output is visually distinct and uses theming/styling consistent with the demo palette.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-325x","title":"demo_showcase: tracing integration","description":"Integrate RichTracingLayer (feature `tracing`) and emit spans/events during the demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:45.281658744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:20.699748573Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","tracing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-325x","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:45.305949215Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-325x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Feature gating

 {"id":"bd-3g9v","title":"demo_showcase: binary scaffolding + scene runner","description":"Add src/bin/demo_showcase.rs with a minimal scene runner, shared Console config, and safe defaults.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:00.036107609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:22.474295129Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scaffold"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3g9v","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:00.049170890Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3g9v","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:22.474267137Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"bd-3g9v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Create the new binary target at `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` and establish the basic runtime structure:
- argument parsing (minimal, dependency-light)
- shared `Console` builder based on CLI flags
- a `Scene` abstraction (name/description/run)
- a deterministic demo clock/sleep helper (scaled by `--speed` / `--quick`)

## Architectural intent

Keep the demo code **mostly self-contained** to avoid file proliferation. Prefer internal modules inside the single bin file:
```rust
mod cli { ... }
mod scenes { ... }
mod ui { ... }
```
(Still a single file, but logically separated.)

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` builds successfully (even without optional features).
- `--help` and `--list-scenes` work.
- Scene runner can run a single scene or full sequence.
- Default behavior auto-disables interactive/live/screen when not a TTY.

## Likely touched files

- `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` (new)
- Potentially `Cargo.toml` only if we decide to add a feature alias like `showcase = ["full","tracing"]` or set `[[bin]].required-features` (decision captured in UX/CLI bead).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3iws","title":"demo_showcase: Pager integration","description":"Integrate Pager for long markdown/runbook output when interactive.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:24.963230792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:45.929678615Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pager"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iws","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:24.977282847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-3iws","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Pager usage

- `--no-interactive` disables pager usage entirely.

-{"id":"bd-3j1s","title":"demo_showcase: timing + deterministic randomness helpers","description":"Implement demo timing helpers (sleep scaling via --quick/--speed) and deterministic seeded randomness.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:08.227855157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:43.037453256Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","timing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j1s","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:08.242030306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-3j1s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why deterministic

Demos often become flaky when they rely on real-time randomness. Determinism makes it:
- screenshot-friendly
- export-friendly
- regression-friendly (humans notice changes)

## Acceptance criteria

- `--quick` reduces total runtime dramatically (e.g., < 3 seconds).
- `--speed` scales sleeps consistently across scenes.
- Any randomness (service IDs, durations, etc.) is seedable (`--seed`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:43Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3j1s","title":"demo_showcase: timing + deterministic randomness helpers","description":"Implement demo timing helpers (sleep scaling via --quick/--speed) and deterministic seeded randomness.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:08.227855157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:58.601793800Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","timing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j1s","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:58.601756130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j1s","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:08.242030306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-3j1s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why deterministic

Demos often become flaky when they rely on real-time randomness. Determinism makes it:
- screenshot-friendly
- export-friendly
- regression-friendly (humans notice changes)

## Acceptance criteria

- `--quick` reduces total runtime dramatically (e.g., < 3 seconds).
- `--speed` scales sleeps consistently across scenes.
- Any randomness (service IDs, durations, etc.) is seedable (`--seed`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jff","title":"demo_showcase: JSON deep-dive scene","description":"Implement the JSON scene (API payloads) using Json renderable and theming.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:14.524790539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:35.845294323Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","json"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jff","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:14.539920501Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-3jff","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Show an API request/response pair aligned with the story, e.g. deploy request payload + status response.

Demonstrate:
- pretty formatting
- theming (JsonTheme if available)
- integration into panels/layout

## Feature gating

If `json` is disabled, show a styled panel with enable instructions.

 {"id":"bd-3m5v","title":"demo_showcase: end-to-end showcase binary (turned up to 11)","description":"Standalone `demo_showcase` binary that showcases rich_rust end-to-end in a cohesive, polished, real-world narrative (all features, turned up to 11).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.204349495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:03.129344912Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase"],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3m5v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Vision

Create a **standalone Rust binary** (`cargo run --bin demo_showcase`) that feels like a real production CLI app, but uses rich_rust to an almost ridiculous degree: polished, stylish, visually dense, and clearly useful.

The goal is not a scattered “feature zoo”. It should read like a cohesive product demo (think: deploy pipeline / ops dashboard / release assistant), with a narrative flow, sensible UI hierarchy, and a few “wow” moments.

## Why this matters

- Proves feature completeness (tables/panels/layout/live/logging/export/etc.) by exercising *everything together* under realistic constraints.
- Provides a high-signal artifact for future refactors (human eyeballs notice regressions immediately).
- Makes the library compelling to adopt: it demonstrates *usefulness*, not just correctness.

## High-level demo flow (expected experience)

1. **Hero screen**: branded title, palette/theme preview, environment capability panel (TTY, color system, width/height).
2. **Dashboard** (Layout): split-screen UI with header + sidebar + main pane.
3. **Live run**: progress bars/spinners + streaming logs; deliberate use of markup, emojis, hyperlinks, and named theme styles.
4. **Deep dives**: Markdown (release notes), Syntax (config/code), JSON (request/response), Pretty/Inspect (structs), Traceback (synthetic error path).
5. **Export**: optional HTML/SVG export bundle (captures output and writes files) with clear paths + instructions.
6. **Wrap-up**: summary table/tree + next steps.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- **Must not hang** in non-interactive contexts (CI / piped output).
- Live/alt-screen/interactive prompts must auto-disable when not a TTY (or via flags).
- Default run should be reasonably quick (configurable via `--speed` / `--quick`).
- Must compile and run cleanly with `--features full,tracing` (and degrade gracefully when features are missing).
- No `unsafe` code anywhere.

## Acceptance criteria (Epic-level)

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing` produces a cohesive, impressive end-to-end demo that clearly exercises: markup/styles/themes, tables, panels, rules, trees, columns/align/padding, layout engine, live updates, progress/spinners, logging (+ tracing if enabled), pretty/inspect, traceback, emoji replacement, hyperlinks, and HTML/SVG export.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` (no features) still runs without crashing; optional scenes display friendly “compile with …” guidance.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase | cat` completes without interactive waits and without runaway ANSI control spam.

## Implementation notes

- Keep the binary self-contained: no network, no external assets required.
- Prefer a small hand-rolled CLI parser (avoid heavy deps).
- Treat this as a *product demo*: strong defaults + flags for power users (`--no-live`, `--screen`, `--export-dir`, etc.).
- Use `Theme` and named styles heavily so the output demonstrates the theming system in a way users can copy.
